title: Paper-guided graph retrieval pruning

queries: graph retrieval pruning soft prompt graph llm neighborhood budget
graph rag subgraph selection graph prompt compression noisy neighborhood

papers.title: G-Retriever: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Graph Question Answering
papers.claim: Compressing too much graph structure into a small prompt channel can lose fidelity, so better subgraph selection can outperform heavier prompt-side mixing.
papers.relevance: Directly motivates pruning the sampled graph context before prompt construction instead of further tuning basis injection coefficients.

papers.title: Graph Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Survey
papers.claim: GraphRAG performance depends strongly on graph indexing and graph-guided retrieval choices, not only on generation-side prompting.
papers.relevance: Supports spending the next Stage 3 slot on the retrieval/pruning corner of the design space.

papers.title: Can we Soft Prompt LLMs for Graph Learning Tasks?
papers.claim: Alignment bottlenecks can come from the graph-text interface itself, so reducing noisy graph inputs can be as important as changing downstream prompt formatting.
papers.relevance: Supports testing a cleaner graph prompt rather than another prompt-decoration or basis-residual tweak.

evidence.why_now: Three consecutive basis-side families kept reproducing the same pattern: regression can improve or stay neutral while classification regresses. That suggests the failure may sit upstream in graph context selection rather than only in basis mixing weights.

hypothesis: The current neighbor budget likely injects too much low-value graph context into the prompt, forcing the basis and projector stack to compress noisy structure. Halving the sampled neighborhood budget for each representative task should yield a cleaner graph prompt and may recover user-churn without giving back the recent user-ltv gains.
notes: Systematic rule for this first graph-pruning scan point: halve the strict-baseline num_neighbors for each representative task while leaving basis, prompt, optimizer, and evaluation settings unchanged.
This is a retrieval-side candidate, not a hyperparameter retune: it changes which graph context reaches the prompt channel.
Representative-task gate remains strict: any primary-metric regression kills the bundle.
Launched and early-killed on 2026-05-05 after the first checkpoint bundle review.
Final report verdict: failed. All three representative tasks were worse than their strict screening baselines.
